_comment: LIVE CONFIG for the guild server. Everything the bot does is controlled from here - no code changes needed. See docs/GUIDE.md for a full explanation of every field.
_comment_audiences: LIVE (go-live July 17, 2026): default=@raiders, teamRed=Raid Team Red, teamBlue=Raid Team Blue by role. The 'testers' audience is PERMANENT - events posted in the test channel only ever DM the testers (Kcin + Mike), never the guild.
_comment_rules: Which audience each event gets, by the channel the signup was posted in. First match wins; no match = default. Red raids Tue + Thu, Blue raids Wed + Sun.
_comment_windows: Hours-before-start at which sign-up reminders fire. 240 = 10 days, because events are posted Tuesday 8PM for the FOLLOWING lockout week: by the Friday summary the Sunday raid is ~9 days (220h) out, so the old 168h window silently dropped it. One reminder per event per person - state.json dedup - sent by the Friday-only schedule in remind.yml.
_comment_horizon: How far ahead the bot LOOKS for events. Does NOT change when reminders send - that is reminder_windows_hours above. 336 = 14 days, so recurring events posted a week or more out (Raid-Helper does this) show up in the log instead of looking like the bot is broken.
_comment_send_hour: Hour (0-23, US Eastern) the Friday digest is allowed to send. remind.yml fires at BOTH 21:00 and 22:00 UTC because GitHub cron cannot follow DST; this pins the actual send to 5PM Eastern year-round by making the wrong one a no-op. Set to null to let any scheduled run send.
_comment_digest: true = members missing from SEVERAL events get ONE combined DM listing them all, instead of one DM per event.

messages.default: Hey {member_name}! You haven't signed up for **{event_title}** yet. It starts {event_time} ({event_time_relative}). Sign up here: {signup_link}
messages.digest_header: Hey {member_name}! You haven't signed up for these upcoming raids yet:
messages.fallback: (couldn't DM you) You have upcoming raids you haven't signed up for - please check the signup channels!

_comment_announcements: Channel messages posted N minutes before each event starts, in the event's own channel. mention_audience=true pings the team that owns the raid (Red raids ping Raid Team Red, Blue ping Raid Team Blue) using the same channel rules as the DMs - so there is no second mapping to maintain. mention_role_ids stays as the fallback if an audience has no roles.

announcements.text: Raid invites has started for tonight's raid! Please have your gear and consumes. Whisper Kcin or an Officer for an invite!

_comment_no_response: Sign-up types that should STILL be nagged. Empty = anyone who clicked anything (Bench/Late/Tentative/Absence) is left alone.
